<<My answer is yes.>> The Python function can be developed using the google-cloud-bigtable client library to extract data from Google Cloud Bigtable. The function would first connect to the Bigtable instance, select the appropriate table, and retrieve the data. Once the data is retrieved, an advanced anomaly detection process can be implemented using machine learning algorithms or statistical methods to identify unusual patterns or outliers in the data. This process would involve preprocessing the data, feature engineering, and applying detection models to flag potential security threats. The function would then return the detected anomalies for further analysis or action. <<cost>>
